name: redis-job-queue-migration
description: Replace the current in-memory job queue with a Redis-backed queue supporting at-least-once delivery, dead-letter handling, and configurable retry. Producers and consumers currently use a shared Map<jobId, Job> in src/queue/memory.ts; migration must preserve the producer/consumer API contract so existing callers continue to compile and function. The goal is durability + horizontal scale (queue survives process restart, multiple consumer instances).
nonGoals: Do not change the producer/consumer API — existing callers must compile without modification
Do not introduce new authentication or credentials beyond the existing REDIS_URL environment variable
Do not remove the in-memory implementation — keep behind a feature flag for rollback
Do not support exactly-once delivery — at-least-once with idempotency_key is sufficient
Do not implement priority queues, scheduled (delayed) jobs, or cron-like features in this project — those are out of scope
constraints: Node.js + TypeScript + ioredis client library (already in package.json)
Integration tests must pass against a real Redis instance (docker-compose fixture)
Cutover must be reversible via feature flag within 5 minutes without data loss
p99 enqueue latency must stay under 10ms against staging Redis
Dead-letter queue must retain failed jobs for 14 days minimum

name: postgres-to-cockroach-migration
description: Migrate the production application database from PostgreSQL 14 to CockroachDB (cloud-hosted) with zero downtime and zero data loss during the cutover. The application uses Prisma ORM across ~40 tables totaling ~2TB, with moderate write throughput (peak 5k writes/sec during business hours). Critical paths include user authentication, billing transactions, and audit log append. Business requirement: complete migration within 6 weeks of project start, with live cutover executed during a 2-hour scheduled maintenance window. Rollback must be possible for 30 days post-cutover.
nonGoals: Do not change the Prisma schema semantics — client-side code must compile and pass tests unchanged
Do not remove PostgreSQL until 30-day post-cutover stability window passes
Do not migrate denormalized read-replica data or analytics pipelines — those are a separate post-cutover project
Do not introduce multi-region writes — single-region CockroachDB only (multi-region is future scope)
Do not disable any existing monitoring, alerting, or logging paths during migration
Do not increase p99 application latency by more than 15% vs PostgreSQL baseline measured over the migration week
Do not modify business logic or application behavior — this is a pure storage-layer migration
constraints: Node.js + TypeScript + Prisma ORM (no raw SQL currently — all queries through Prisma client)
CockroachDB Cloud Serverless tier for staging, Dedicated tier for production
Dual-write period required for data validation before cutover (write to both databases, read from Postgres, compare)
Migration tooling must support schema diff + data sync with throughput sufficient for 2TB over the 2-hour window
Rollback plan must be tested end-to-end in staging before production cutover
Audit log table has foreign-key constraints on 12 other tables — coordinate schema migration ordering
PII-containing tables (users, payments) require additional data validation checks + encryption-at-rest verification
CockroachDB has known differences from PostgreSQL in transaction semantics (serializable by default) — audit affected code paths
Billing transactions are financial — zero data loss is a hard requirement, not a target
